steps.title: Understand what 'distinct values' means for x-intercepts
steps.content: An x-intercept is a value of $x$ where $f(x) = 0$. The question asks for the number of distinct $x$-values where this happens — repeated roots (where the graph touches the axis without crossing) count only once.

steps.title: Count the distinct x-intercepts on the graph
steps.content: From the graph: the curve touches the x-axis at one point (tangent — a double root) and crosses it at one other point. That gives two distinct values of $x$ where $f(x) = 0$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
